name: Mirva-Rabanal Trail
name_original: Valles de Mirva y Rabanal – trail running circular desde Burón (Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre)
description: Jog the Mirva-Rabanal Trail, a difficult 8.0-mile route through diverse forests and valleys with stunning Picos de Europa views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Burón Spain offer diverse terrain within a landscape characterized by dramatic mountain vistas and the unique "León fjords." The region is situated within or near the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park, providing access to an extensive network of trails. Runners can explore a variety of natural features, including high peaks like Pico Gilbo, serene lakes, and deep canyons, all contributing to a dynamic environment for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Burón The most popular jogging route is Pico Gilbo Circular Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the iconic Pico Gilbo, often called the "Leonese Matterhorn." Another top favourite among local runners is Mirva-Rabanal Trail, a difficult 8.0 miles (12.8 km) path. This route traverses varied mountain terrain, providing a challenging and rewarding running experience. Local runners also love the Refugio de Vegabaño - trail running round trip from Puerto de Panderrueda through the Picos de Europa National Park, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail leading through the Picos de Europa National Park, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Jogging around Burón is defined by mountain vistas, "León fjords," and the extensive network of trails within the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park. The region offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to challenging mountain running. The routes in Burón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Burón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Burón?
A: There are nearly 40 dedicated running routes around Burón, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The region is particularly known for its challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Burón?
A: Jogging routes around Burón feature diverse terrain, from dramatic mountain vistas and the unique 'León fjords' to serene lakes and deep canyons. Many trails are within or near the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park, providing varied landscapes for an engaging running experience.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Burón?
A: While Burón is primarily known for its more challenging mountain runs, there is at least one easy running route available. For those new to trail running or looking for a less strenuous option, it's advisable to check the route details for elevation and distance before heading out.

Q: Can I find challenging long-distance running trails in Burón?
A: Yes, Burón is an excellent destination for challenging long-distance running. Many routes are classified as difficult, with significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo of Salio - Riaño – Hoz Oscura loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; covers over 22 km with substantial ascent, offering a demanding experience for seasoned runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Burón?
A: Yes, several circular running routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055978834/pico-gilbo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilbo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, offering stunning views of the iconic Pico Gilbo. Another challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yordas (1,967 m) loop from Liegos&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to a high peak.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks I can see while running in Burón?
A: Burón's running routes offer access to breathtaking viewpoints and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5890955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tombo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides impressive vistas of the Cares Gorge and the Picos de Europa. You can also run near the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/306845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilbo Peak&lt;/a&gt;, often called the 'Leonese Matterhorn,' which offers spectacular panoramic views of the Riaño Reservoir.

Q: Are there any mountain huts or refuges accessible from the running trails?
A: Yes, some running routes pass by or lead to mountain huts. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052051580/refugio-de-vegabano-trail-running-round-trip-from-puerto-de-panderrueda-through-the-picos-de-europa-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de Vegabaño - trail running round trip from Puerto de Panderrueda through the Picos de Europa National Park&lt;/a&gt; is a route that includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5146641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegabaño Mountain Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, offering a potential stop or destination during your run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Burón?
A: The running routes in Burón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 3 reviews. Runners often praise the dramatic mountain scenery, the challenging nature of the trails, and the diverse natural features found throughout the region.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the 'León fjords' or the Riaño reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its stunning mountain vistas and the unique 'León fjords,' particularly around the Riaño reservoir. Many routes, including the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055978834/pico-gilbo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilbo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offer spectacular panoramic views of these geological formations.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Burón?
A: Burón's diverse natural features, including mountains and fjords, make it suitable for running across different seasons. However, given the mountainous terrain and potential for challenging conditions, spring and autumn generally offer more temperate weather for comfortable running. Winter can bring snow and ice, making some high-altitude trails more difficult or inaccessible.

Q: Are there any specific natural features like waterfalls or canyons along the running routes?
A: Beyond prominent mountains and fjords, Burón's surroundings boast a rich variety of natural elements, including serene lakes, dramatic canyons, and cascading waterfalls. While specific routes to waterfalls aren't highlighted, the general landscape within the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park suggests opportunities to encounter such features.
tips:
The trail between Burón and Retuerto, passing through Mirva, is part of a beautiful hiking route in the Riaño and Mampodre Mountains, in the province of León.
Beech forests, meadows, and glacial valleys.
Panoramic views of the central and western Picos de Europa mountain ranges.
Retuerto, a charming little town with plenty of opportunities for relaxation.
The Burón to Retuerto Beech Forest Trail is a hiking route that crosses one of the most beautiful beech forests in the Riaño Mountains, in the province of León.

Beautiful panoramic view of the western and central massifs of the Picos de Europa. Down in the valley, we will see the town of Retuerto. About 100 meters away there is a forest hut that also offers us great views.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Mirva-Rabanal Trail?
faqs.answer: For jogging, the Mirva-Rabanal Trail typically takes around 1 hour and 43 minutes to complete. This is for the full circular route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: The Mirva-Rabanal Trail is considered difficult for jogging. While some initial sections are gentle, it features very steep climbs through beech forests and pronounced descents that demand a moderate to high level of endurance. Trekking poles can be helpful for stability on descents.

faqs.question: Is the Mirva-Rabanal Trail a loop, or is it an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Mirva-Rabanal Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter soft leaf litter in forested areas, tall green grasses in open sections, and potentially muddy areas, especially in the Rabanal valley. There are zigzagging descents through beech forests that can be slippery, and you might cross small streams. The route also includes sections of green meadows, dense forests of beech and oak, and a short segment on a paved road near the end.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse and beautiful scenery, often described as 'fantasy-like'. You'll pass through lush green meadows, impressive beech forests with moss-covered rocks, and enjoy panoramic views. Key highlights include the Mirva Valley, the Burón–Retuerto Trail through the Beech Forest, and spectacular vistas of the Picos de Europa from viewpoints like the View of the Picos de Europa and the Retuerto Valley. Don't miss the 'most beautiful bench in Burón' near the end for exceptional views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Mirva-Rabanal Trail?
faqs.answer: Early summer is particularly charming due to abundant wildflowers and verdant pastures. However, the trail's diverse landscapes are beautiful throughout spring, summer, and autumn. Be mindful of potentially slippery conditions in wet weather or during leaf fall.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Mirva-Rabanal Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Burón. While specific parking details aren't provided, you would typically look for parking within or on the outskirts of Burón to begin your journey.

faqs.question: Is the Mirva-Rabanal Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and livestock.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this trail or the regional park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Mirva-Rabanal Trail or the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park. It's open for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Mirva-Rabanal Trail (PR-LE 21) is a well-marked route. It also passes through sections of the 'Camino Burón-Retuerto por Mirva'.

faqs.question: Are there any specific strategies or insider tips for jogging this route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'very steep climb' and 'very pronounced descents', consider tackling the route in the direction that best suits your preference for ascents versus descents. Some find it easier to get the steepest climb out of the way early. Also, trekking poles can significantly aid stability on the more challenging downhill sections.

waypoints.name: Burón–Retuerto Trail through the Beech Forest
View of the Picos de Europa and the Retuerto Valley
